jokes.text: Big Pink! The gum with the breath-freshening power of ham.
jokes.setup: Standard commercial for breath-freshening gum
jokes.punchline: Ham as a breath freshener - completely absurd and backwards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy - ham being breath-freshening is hilariously backwards logic delivered with straight commercial confidence

jokes.text: And it pinkens your teeth while you chew.
jokes.setup: Following the ham breath-freshening claim
jokes.punchline: Pinkening teeth as a selling point - opposite of whitening
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up absurdist beat - pink teeth as desirable continues the backwards commercial logic

jokes.text: What? This is the worst kind of discrimination! The kind against me!
jokes.setup: Being told military discount doesn't apply to civilians
jokes.punchline: Bender's narcissistic definition of discrimination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - his self-centered worldview makes personal discrimination the 'worst kind'

jokes.text: if for any reason you're not satisfied, I hate you.
jokes.setup: Standard customer service satisfaction guarantee
jokes.punchline: Hostile twist on traditional guarantee language
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - subverts customer service expectation with perfect deadpan hostility

jokes.text: Full price for gum? That dog won't hunt, Monsignor.
jokes.setup: Fry getting angry about paying full price
jokes.punchline: Mixing folksy saying with formal religious title
jokes.explanation: Good Fry character beat - his tendency to mix incompatible language styles for comic effect

jokes.text: Playing you all for chumps? Correct. There's no obligation.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if they can exploit the system
jokes.punchline: Recruiter honestly confirming they're being played
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony - the recruiter's casual honesty about military deception is perfectly matter-of-fact

jokes.text: Unless, of course, war were declared.
jokes.setup: Reassuring them there's no obligation
jokes.punchline: The one crucial exception delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff - the casual delivery of the one exception that changes everything

jokes.text: What's that? War were declared.
jokes.setup: Characters didn't hear the war declaration
jokes.punchline: Casual repetition as alarms blare and chaos erupts
jokes.explanation: Iconic Futurama moment - the casual delivery contrasted with immediate wartime chaos is perfect

jokes.text: to gain their courage. Their rich, tasty courage.
jokes.setup: Military advice about eating enemy hearts
jokes.punchline: Describing courage as a flavor with culinary enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp character moment - his pompous military persona mixed with absurd culinary enthusiasm

jokes.text: I don't want you to worry about your jobs. That's why I'm firing you now.
jokes.setup: Reassuring employees about job security
jokes.punchline: Immediate firing as the 'solution' to worry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Professor character logic - his twisted kindness where firing is presented as consideration

jokes.text: After some deadly blunders caused by distracting low-cut fatigues and harmless pinching, the Army decided women weren't fit.
jokes.setup: Explaining why women can't serve in the army
jokes.punchline: Blaming women for men's inability to focus and behave
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp character moment revealing his sexist logic and complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: If it's a lesson in love, I suffer from a very sexy learning disability.
jokes.setup: Leela threatening to teach him a lesson
jokes.punchline: Zapp turning disability into a sexual boast
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp - his ability to turn anything into sexual braggadocio, complete with made-up condition

jokes.text: What do I call it, Kif? Sex-lexia.
jokes.setup: Zapp asking for the name of his 'condition'
jokes.punchline: Dyslexia pun mixing sex with learning disability
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - 'sexlexia' became a quotable Futurama reference, perfect Zapp/Kif dynamic

jokes.text: A few will be forced through a fine mesh screen. They'll be the luckiest.
jokes.setup: Describing various fates of soldiers
jokes.punchline: Gruesome death described as lucky compared to alternatives
jokes.explanation: Dark military humor escalating to absurd extremes - mesh screen death as 'lucky' is perfectly twisted

jokes.text: And this ham gum is all bones.
jokes.setup: Complaining about their situation
jokes.punchline: Ham gum having bones - extending the earlier absurd product
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the ham gum - bones in gum extends the absurdist product concept

jokes.text: welcome the original Gerber Baby. Earth President Richard M. Nixon.
jokes.setup: Introducing the mission briefing presenter
jokes.punchline: Nixon as the Gerber Baby - visual absurdity of baby Nixon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual absurdity - Nixon as Gerber Baby is peak Futurama surreal character design

jokes.text: Cool effect!
jokes.setup: Hologram of planet being displayed
jokes.punchline: Fry commenting on the show's own special effects
jokes.explanation: Light meta moment - Fry breaking fourth wall to comment on animation effects

jokes.text: Why is this planet worth dying for? Can't say, you're the one who'll die.
jokes.setup: Soldier asking about mission importance
jokes.punchline: Nixon's callous logic about information and expendability
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor about military expendability - Nixon's brutal honesty is perfectly in character

jokes.text: They stand for everything we don't stand for.
jokes.setup: Explaining who the enemy is despite knowing nothing
jokes.punchline: Completely circular definition that says nothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of military propaganda - meaningless enemy definition that sounds authoritative

jokes.text: Also, they said you look like dorks! They look like dorks! I'm gonna--
jokes.setup: Nixon trying to motivate troops against enemy
jokes.punchline: Playground insults as military motivation, Fry taking the bait
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from meaningless ideology to childish name-calling as war justification

jokes.text: Tent got your tongue? Kif, write that down and send it to Humor in Uniform.
jokes.setup: Soldier struggling with tent setup
jokes.punchline: Cat got your tongue pun, plus Zapp thinking he's hilarious
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp character moment - his pride in terrible wordplay and military magazine submission

jokes.text: That guy makes Speedy Gonzales look like Regular Gonzales.
jokes.setup: Commenting on someone's extreme speed
jokes.punchline: Speedy Gonzales compared to hypothetical 'Regular Gonzales'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation formula - taking the fastest character and making him look slow by comparison

jokes.text: And 16 minutes. And 12 hours.
jokes.setup: Zapp claiming someone beat his record by two seconds
jokes.punchline: Kif's deadpan revelation of how much Zapp actually lost by
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif/Zapp dynamic - Kif's deadpan correction reveals Zapp's massive incompetence

jokes.text: As a bubbling Crock-Pot of male hormones, I sure do.
jokes.setup: Leela (as Lee Lemon) mentioning testosterone
jokes.punchline: Zapp's ridiculous self-description as kitchen appliance full of hormones
jokes.explanation: Peak Zapp absurdity - comparing himself to a kitchen appliance while bragging about masculinity

jokes.text: Lee, La, Man. La Man. Lemon! Lee Lemon, sir.
jokes.setup: Zapp asking for Leela's (disguised) name
jokes.punchline: Leela struggling to come up with a male name on the spot
jokes.explanation: Good disguise comedy - Leela's panic leading to awkward name construction

jokes.text: your hand, while firm and manly, is soft as a velvet child.
jokes.setup: Zapp commenting on Lee Lemon's handshake
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to 'velvet child' as compliment
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Zapp moment - 'velvet child' is such a bizarre and uncomfortable comparison

jokes.text: Squirt it on some homeless man with dry elbows.
jokes.setup: Disposing of his old lotion for better stuff
jokes.punchline: Casual disposal involving homeless person and specific body part
jokes.explanation: Dark Zapp humor - his callous treatment of people as disposal methods with oddly specific detail

jokes.text: And some other emotions that are weird and deeply confusing me.
jokes.setup: Zapp saying Lee Lemon fills him with hope
jokes.punchline: His confusion about feeling attraction to what he thinks is a man
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows why Zapp is confused while he doesn't understand his attraction

jokes.text: Mind if we sit with you? Why the hell would I?
jokes.setup: Polite request to join someone
jokes.punchline: Bender's logical but rude response to rhetorical question
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - taking rhetorical politeness literally and responding with selfish logic

jokes.text: A foul-mouthed, barrel-chested, beer-bellied pile of ugly muscle.
jokes.setup: Bender saying Fry is his kind of soldier
jokes.punchline: Increasingly insulting description presented as compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - his version of bonding involves increasingly harsh 'compliments'

jokes.text: Stop flattering me!
jokes.setup: Continued insults from Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender interpreting insults as flattery
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender ego moment - his ability to interpret anything as praise for himself

jokes.text: It's about life in ancient Greece.
jokes.setup: Zapp offering Lee Lemon a book on tape
jokes.punchline: The implication of what kind of 'Greek' book he's offering
jokes.explanation: Subtle adult humor - Zapp's attempt at seduction through historical 'literature'

jokes.text: Surprise!
jokes.setup: Explaining the element of surprise is key to victory
jokes.punchline: Immediately shouting 'Surprise!' ruining any surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp incompetence - immediately defeating his own strategy with maximum efficiency

jokes.text: And then the battle's not so bad? Right, I forgot about the battle.
jokes.setup: Bender saying battle calm is the worst part
jokes.punchline: His realization he forgot about the actual battle being worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation logic - the pre-battle anxiety being worse until remembering the actual battle

jokes.text: The enemy! They're balls!
jokes.setup: First sight of the enemy
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic revelation that enemies are just balls
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reveal - the mysterious enemy being simple geometric shapes is perfectly Futurama

jokes.text: You spooked Felicity.
jokes.setup: Fry accidentally firing his weapon
jokes.punchline: Zapp's gun having a feminine name like a pet
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp character detail - his tendency to personify and feminize his equipment

jokes.text: Wait. I didn't tell you where he lives. Your son might also like those boots.
jokes.setup: Soldier giving Fry something for his son
jokes.punchline: Realizing the logical problem, then adding boots as afterthought
jokes.explanation: Good dying soldier cliche subversion - the practical problem with deathbed requests

jokes.text: Fry, you emu-bellied coward!
jokes.setup: Bender insulting Fry's cowardice
jokes.punchline: Specific and unusual animal-based body insult
jokes.explanation: Good Bender insult creativity - emu-belly is oddly specific and visual

jokes.text: If they put me on a stamp, tell them to use the young Bender.
jokes.setup: Bender's dying words after being shot
jokes.punchline: Vanity concern about his appearance on commemorative stamp
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender death scene - completely self-absorbed even while dying

jokes.text: If they put me on a stamp, tell them to use the young Bender.
jokes.setup: Bender apparently dying heroically
jokes.punchline: His final concern being his appearance on commemorative stamps
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - even in 'death' his vanity and ego are the primary concern

jokes.text: I mean, five! Repeat, four.
jokes.setup: Calling for medical personnel to tent four
jokes.punchline: Immediately correcting to five, then back to four - tent confusion
jokes.explanation: Basic confusion comedy - medical emergency hampered by simple organizational failure

jokes.text: I'd love to. But first, I have to perform surgery. I kid. I kid.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he's ready to operate
jokes.punchline: Making it sound like he'd rather do something else, then revealing it's a joke
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg moment - his attempt at humor during serious medical situation

jokes.text: Scalpel. Blood bucket. Priest. Next patient!
jokes.setup: Standard medical procedure requests
jokes.punchline: Escalation from surgical tools to priest implies patient died quickly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation - perfect economy showing Zoidberg's terrible medical skills through surgical requests

jokes.text: Leave some for the enemy to kill.
jokes.setup: Complaint about Zoidberg killing patients
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion to leave patients for the enemy instead
jokes.explanation: Good dark war humor - the idea that enemy killing would be preferable to their own doctor

jokes.text: Yeah, he's a doctor and a butcher.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Zoidberg's qualifications
jokes.punchline: Literal butcher profession combined with medical incompetence
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on Zoidberg's medical incompetence - doctor/butcher double meaning

jokes.text: Being your assistant. Wrong! Being your assistant.
jokes.setup: Zapp asking what the most humiliating job is
jokes.punchline: Kif's honest answer being dismissed then repeated as the answer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Zapp/Kif dynamic - Zapp's complete obliviousness to Kif's subtle burn

jokes.text: Excuse me, I believe I'm the doctor. Believing it won't make it true.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asserting his medical authority
jokes.punchline: Bender's philosophical burn about belief vs reality
jokes.explanation: Sharp philosophical burn - Bender dismissing Zoidberg's credentials with brutal logic

jokes.text: It isn't war, it's murder!
jokes.setup: Fry's earlier statement about the war
jokes.punchline: Repeated with different emphasis for dramatic effect
jokes.explanation: Basic dramatic repetition - not particularly clever but fits Fry's traumatized state

jokes.text: I want this robot fixed. Fixed like Kennedy fixed the 1960 election.
jokes.setup: Ordering Bender to be repaired
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'fixed' - repaired vs rigged election
jokes.explanation: Good historical wordplay - fixed meaning both repaired and rigged works well

jokes.text: That's my joke! I'll kill you!
jokes.setup: Someone else using Zoidberg's exact surgery line
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's violent reaction to joke theft
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - Zoidberg's pride in his one joke leading to violence

jokes.text: I got wheels! With clickity-clackers!
jokes.setup: Kif arriving at officers' club in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: His childlike excitement about wheelchair features
jokes.explanation: Good Kif moment - finding joy in small things despite being wounded

jokes.text: I got wheels! With clickity-clackers!
jokes.setup: Bender being repaired/modified
jokes.punchline: His childish excitement about wheelchair accessories
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - his ability to find joy in mechanical modifications

jokes.text: And mix these mixed nuts! I see two almonds touching!
jokes.setup: Zapp giving Kif menial tasks
jokes.punchline: Obsessive control over nut arrangement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp micromanagement - his petty tyranny over completely trivial details

jokes.text: So I am sending you on one last mission. Hot diggity daffodil!
jokes.setup: Nixon assigning important peace mission
jokes.punchline: Zapp's inappropriately childish exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp characterization - childish enthusiasm for serious mission

jokes.text: Looking like that, he talked his way into Jill St. John's bed.
jokes.setup: Questioning if Henry Kissinger is any good at negotiation
jokes.punchline: His unlikely romantic success despite appearance
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference humor - Kissinger's unlikely romantic reputation

jokes.text: I didn't live 1000 years to look at another man's gizmo.
jokes.setup: Nixon accidentally seeing Zapp naked
jokes.punchline: Nixon's age making the situation worse plus euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good Nixon character moment - his age and prudishness with euphemistic language

jokes.text: Kif, raise him up nipple high.
jokes.setup: Nixon wanting to address Zapp properly
jokes.punchline: Specific anatomical height reference as military protocol
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific absurdity - nipple height as official military measurement

jokes.text: Number 10: 'CHUMP', Number 9: 'CHUMPETTE'...
jokes.setup: Reading Bender's most used words to find the bomb trigger
jokes.punchline: The progression of Bender's typical vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character integration - his vocabulary perfectly captured in countdown form

jokes.text: What do you call it? Lower back! That whole region.
jokes.setup: About to say 'pain in the ass' which would trigger bomb
jokes.punchline: Awkward circumlocution to avoid saying the word
jokes.explanation: Good tension/comedy balance - the awkward avoidance of the trigger word creates both suspense and humor

jokes.text: My chair's too hard. It's a pain in the-- What do you call it? Lower back! That whole region.
jokes.setup: Kissinger about to say the trigger word 'ass'
jokes.punchline: Awkward circumvention to avoid saying the dangerous word
jokes.explanation: Good tension/relief moment - the near miss with the bomb trigger word

jokes.text: I did it! Wait, that's not me.
jokes.setup: Fry apparently succeeding at heroic action
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing someone else did it
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment - brief moment of pride immediately deflated

jokes.text: Fry, don't you recognize me? Hermes?
jokes.setup: Fry surprised someone wants to save Bender
jokes.punchline: Reveal that it's actually Hermes in disguise, not the expected character
jokes.explanation: Good disguise reveal moment - unexpected character caring about Bender

jokes.text: So it's you I've been attracted to! I've never been so happy to be beaten up by a woman.
jokes.setup: Zapp discovering Lee Lemon is actually Leela
jokes.punchline: His relief about his attraction plus masochistic happiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - his relief about heterosexuality plus his odd relationship with violence

jokes.text: Then he bounced 4 meters in the air. Do I make myself clear?
jokes.setup: Elder telling story about legendary ball bouncing
jokes.punchline: Treating random bouncing heights as profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good alien culture humor - their mythology being completely meaningless bouncing statistics

jokes.text: Where do you shove things up a ball? Not a productive area of discussion.
jokes.setup: Nixon threatening to shove treaty up enemy ball
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility acknowledged matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - the practical problems of threatening spherical beings

jokes.text: Where do you shove things up a ball? Not a productive area of discussion.
jokes.setup: Nixon wanting to threaten the ball aliens
jokes.punchline: Kissinger's diplomatic deflection of anatomically impossible threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - the practical impossibility of threatening spherical beings combined with diplomatic understatement

jokes.text: At last, war has made me into a man.
jokes.setup: Fry parachuting into danger to save Bender
jokes.punchline: Classic war movie cliche delivered at wrong moment
jokes.explanation: Good cliche subversion - Fry's heroic moment undermined by timing and context

jokes.text: At last, war has made me into a man.
jokes.setup: Fry overcoming his cowardice
jokes.punchline: Classic war movie cliche delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Standard war movie parody - not particularly original but fits Fry's character arc

jokes.text: We've seen too many body bags and ball sacks.
jokes.setup: Peace negotiation speech about war casualties
jokes.punchline: Mixing serious war terminology with ball alien anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay mixing serious war terminology with the absurdity of ball anatomy

jokes.text: Up yours, chump! I said it 906 times before lunch.
jokes.setup: Being told he can't say 'ass' or the planet explodes
jokes.punchline: Bender's defiance with specific count of previous usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender defiance - his specific count shows both his nature and the absurdity

jokes.text: Young man, you have the bravery of a hero. And breath as fresh as a summer ham.
jokes.setup: Elder praising Fry's heroism
jokes.punchline: Callback to the ham breath freshener from opening
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening gum commercial - ham as breath freshener comes full circle

jokes.text: The game's name is: Make Bender Happy, or He Blows Up the Planet!
jokes.setup: Bender realizing his power with the bomb
jokes.punchline: His immediate exploitation of the situation for personal gain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - immediately turning global crisis into personal benefit game

jokes.text: Stay calm. No need to bounce off the handle.
jokes.setup: Diplomatic attempt to calm situation
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'fly off the handle' adapted for ball aliens
jokes.explanation: Decent pun adapting common idiom for ball alien context

jokes.text: I guess you learned a valuable lesson. Don't mess with Earth!
jokes.setup: Earth accidentally conquering the alien planet
jokes.punchline: Fry treating accidental victory as intentional intimidation
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Fry's misunderstanding of how they won the war

jokes.text: It's stuck in there with glue or something.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they couldn't remove the bomb
jokes.punchline: Incredibly casual and vague explanation for life-threatening device
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor - treating highly advanced bomb as mundane problem with amateur diagnosis

jokes.text: Great. What's the point of living if I can't say 'ass'? I didn't blow up! Ass, ass, ass, ass!
jokes.setup: Bender learning he can't say his favorite word
jokes.punchline: His existential crisis followed by immediate joy at saying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his priorities and immediate celebration of swearing freedom

jokes.text: That's using your ass. And the word? Better if you don't know.
jokes.setup: Bender asking what the new bomb trigger word is
jokes.punchline: Professor accidentally using 'ass' in different context, then refusing to reveal word
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'ass' in different context - brain vs body part

jokes.text: Wait. Is it 'sorry'? No. 'Funderful'?
jokes.setup: Bender trying to guess the new trigger word
jokes.punchline: 'Funderful' as made-up word Bender would never say
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - 'funderful' is perfectly chosen as something Bender would never sincerely say

jokes.text: 'Compassion.' 'Shrimp toast.' 'Antiquing'?
jokes.setup: Bender guessing words he never says
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and un-Bender-like words
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - the escalating absurdity of words Bender would never say
